title: Marian Ilitch - Crain's Detroit Business
text:
Marian Ilitch - Crain's Detroit Business

Skip to main content

## 2012

2012

Read more Marian Ilitch

View All

Share

Owner, **MotorCity Casino Hotel** , Detroit

**Why she's a game changer**

Co-founded **Little Caesar Enterprises Inc.** with husband Mike in 1959. Her financial expertise balanced his marketing savvy, and the two built a family of companies that posted $2.4 billion last year. She broke out on her own in 2005 when she became full owner of the MotorCity Casino Hotel.

• **Résumé:** Started the Little Caesar empire with her husband, Mike. Now is co-owner of the Little Caesar pizza chain, **Blue Line Foodservice Distribution** , the **Detroit Red Wings** , **Olympia Entertainment** , **Olympia Development** , **Little Caesars Pizza Kits Fundraising Program** , **Champion Foods** and **Uptown Entertainment** . Mike Ilitch owns the **Detroit Tigers** .

• **Education:** Fordson High School, Dearborn; Dearborn Community College (several classes)

Marian Ilitch has always worked hard, starting with her first job at age 10 filling salt and pepper shakers at her dad's restaurant.

Her hard work continued when she and her husband started a lonely restaurant in Garden City, particularly when she had to tell Mike Ilitch to stop giving away dinners.

And while it would seem that starting a family business was the pivotal point in her career, it wasn't.

That came a few years later, when she had to decide if she was going to be the full-time accountant for their growing company, or stay at home with her three children.

"I didn't recognize how important it was at the time that I made that decision to stop working out of the house and go work full time in the office," Ilitch said. "That decision was a pivotal point in my life."

Especially in the early years of her career, balancing work and home life was difficult. Her three kids became seven, and the responsibility of a growing family constantly competed against a growing company for her attention.

"I promised myself I'd handle both jobs, even if it meant losing sleep and doing work after the kids went to bed. And I promised myself I'd never complain, because I was petrified that Mike would want me to go home if it got to be too much," she said.

"That's what makes it such a different deal for women. I wanted to be a mother, and I wanted to work. I wanted to do both so badly."

She credits the perfect balance of personalities between herself and Mike for their success. Their combined holdings -- estimated at $2.4 billion by _Forbes_ \-- include **Little Caesar Enterprises Inc.** , the **Detroit Tigers** and the **Detroit Red Wings** . Marian is sole owner of **MotorCity Casino Hotel** .

"None of this would be possible for me without his love and support over the years," she said.

To demonstrate how she and her husband have allocated responsibility in the company over the years, Marian recounted the oft-told story about the opening of their first restaurant, **Little Caesars Pizza Treat** , in Garden City in 1959.

He gave away a free fish dinner to the first customer who walked through the door after they opened, celebrating their first dinner served. He gave away the second dinner as well, celebrating that it was the first chicken dinner served.

When a third customer came in, ordering a pizza, Marian Ilitch said she caught a look from Mike and then jumped in, worried there would be nothing in the cash register.

"Before he could get a word out, I said, "That will be $3, please.' "

It exemplifies the dynamic between the two, she said.

"Mike is the marketing person, and he's always had his eye on the opportunities out there," she said. "I'm the one who does what we'll call the due diligence. And that's worked extremely well."

Marian Ilitch's knowledge of finance and business has come from the discipline of taking the time to learn from those around her. She declined to name people, noting that there are so many.

"Not a day has gone by when I didn't learn something from someone," she said. "If they can do it better than me, I can learn something from them. That's how I've always done it, and I started from the ground up."

At the heart of her learning, she said, is common sense.

"I'm blessed with common sense, and it took me awhile to realize that not everyone has that," she joked.

Listening to common sense is something that she urged other people to do in their careers. Other advice was to accept challenges and work hard.

"I'm an achiever. I think of myself as an achiever first, and as a woman second," Ilitch said.

She said people should be honest with themselves, strive for perfection and take risks.

Her mistakes have been small enough that they didn't pose a major challenge to the business, she said.

"But you have to make those mistakes," she said. "If you think you won't make mistakes, you're in a dream world. And you're never going to be a risk-taker if you don't."

One of those risks was taken in 2005 when Ilitch became full owner of MotorCity Casino Hotel.

It was a decision point: Sell her share to the other partners in the deal, or buy them out.

"They all wanted to sell, and I was the perfect person to step in," she said.

Ilitch went from 25 percent owner of the casino to full owner in 2005 after she bought **Mandalay Resort Group Inc.** 's 53.5 percent share for $525 million. She also bought the 11.5 percent share from **Atwater Associates LLC** for $100 million and the 10 percent share from investor Thomas Celani for an undisclosed sum.

Running a casino has tested everything she knows about entertainment, hospitality and finance, she said.

"It's an intense, competitive business," she said, "much more so than people might think."

Learning another business has been the take-away for Ilitch, and it's been driving her interest to grow her casino holdings.

At 79, she has no plans to retire.

Someone recently asked her when she's going to "stop and smell the roses," Ilitch said.

"I told her, "Hey, I buy a dozen roses and put them on my desk every week. That's when I smell the roses.' "
